title: Streaming & Broadcast Response
interaction_flow_stream: Client → Promptable: stream(prompt, attachments, provider, model, timeout)
Promptable → TextProvider: stream(new AgentPrompt(agent, prompt, attachments, provider, model, timeout))
StreamsText → StreamableAgentResponse: new StreamableAgentResponse(invocationId, generatorClosure, meta)
StreamsText → TextGateway: streamText(invocationId, provider, model, instructions, messages, tools, options, timeout)
TextGateway → AI API: HTTP streaming request
AI API → TextGateway: yield raw SSE chunks
TextGateway → StreamsText: yield StreamEvent (TextDelta | StreamEnd | StreamStart)
StreamableAgentResponse → Client: yield StreamEvent per chunk
StreamableAgentResponse → StreamableAgentResponse: collect events, build text & usage (after all chunks)
StreamableAgentResponse → StreamedAgentResponse: new StreamedAgentResponse(invocationId, events, meta)
StreamsText (then) → EventDispatcher: dispatch(new AgentStreamed(invocationId, prompt, response))
interaction_flow_broadcast: Client → Promptable: broadcast(prompt, channels, attachments, now=false)
Promptable → Promptable: stream(prompt, attachments, provider, model) [same as stream flow]
Promptable (callback) → StreamEvent: broadcast(channels) OR broadcastNow(channels) [based on 'now' flag]
LaravelBroadcast → WebSocket/Channel: push event payload to subscribers
interaction_flow_broadcast_now: Promptable → Promptable: broadcast(prompt, channels, attachments, now=true)
interaction_flow_broadcast_on_queue: Client → Promptable: broadcastOnQueue(prompt, channels, attachments, provider, model)
Promptable → BroadcastAgent: BroadcastAgent::dispatch(agent, prompt, channels, attachments, provider, model)
BroadcastAgent (queue worker) → Agent: stream(prompt, attachments, provider, model)
interaction_flow_http_response: StreamableAgentResponse → HttpResponse: response()->stream(fn: foreach events → yield 'data: {json}\n\n')

methods: textProviderFor(Agent $agent, ?string $name): TextProvider [injects fake gateway if faked]
stream(AgentPrompt $prompt): StreamableAgentResponse [generates invocationId, runs pipeline, dispatches events, wraps TextGateway::streamText in generator closure]
then(callable $callback): self [called with StreamedAgentResponse after full stream]
getIterator(): Traversable [calls generator, yields StreamEvents, builds text/usage]

key_relationships: Promptable (trait) uses AiManager::textProviderFor() to resolve TextProvider per agent
Promptable::broadcast() delegates to stream() then calls each() on StreamableAgentResponse
BroadcastAgent::handle() calls agent.stream() then broadcastNow per event (synchronous within queue)
TextProvider (concrete class) uses StreamsText trait which implements stream()
StreamsText::stream() wraps TextGateway::streamText() in a lazy generator closure
StreamableAgentResponse::getIterator() materializes the generator on first iteration
StreamEvent::broadcast() delegates to Laravel Broadcast facade (queued or immediate)
AiManager extends MultipleInstanceManager; resolves concrete providers by driver name from config
important_notes: Stream is LAZY: TextGateway::streamText() is not called until StreamableAgentResponse is iterated
broadcast() is SYNCHRONOUS streaming: iterates SSE chunks inline and broadcasts each one
broadcastOnQueue() is ASYNC: pushes job to queue; job then streams and broadcasts synchronously within worker
Failover: withModelFailover() retries with next provider/model on FailoverableException, fires AgentFailedOver event
Provider/model resolution: checks $provider param → agent->provider() method → @Provider attribute → config('ai.default')
Model resolution: checks $model param → agent->model() method → @Model attribute → UseSmartestModel/UseCheapestModel attributes → provider->defaultTextModel()
Timeout: checks $timeout param → agent->timeout() method → @Timeout attribute → default 60s
Events fired: StreamingAgent (before stream starts), AgentStreamed (after full stream completes)
StreamableAgentResponse implements Responsable: toResponse() returns SSE text/event-stream response or Vercel AI SDK protocol
Vercel protocol support: usingVercelDataProtocol() switches serialization format for Vercel AI SDK compatibility
then() callbacks receive a StreamedAgentResponse with full events collection, combined text, and usage
